observations.note: Bash script security practices excellent: proper quoting, jq for JSON parsing (no shell concat), counter file 0600 permissions, graceful degradation on errors
Function decomposition professional: log(), find_project_root(), read_counter(), increment_counter() all ≤25 lines, single responsibility, reusable

Error handling exceeds expectations: trap on unexpected errors (exit 0), malformed JSON handled gracefully (line 90-92), missing jq detected early (line 33-36), counter corruption detected (line 128-131)
Test coverage 100% (64/64 tests passing): 6 AC files + 3 edge cases + 1 integration E2E. All acceptance criteria verified. Counter behavior tested across invocations.
Settings.json integration correct: Stop event registered, 15-second timeout appropriate for 100+ file scanning, existing hooks preserved (PreToolUse, PostToolUse, PermissionRequest unchanged)
Loop prevention pattern elegant: early exit on stop_hook_active (line 63) prevents expensive scanning, counter increment per block (line 154), max=3 balances enforcement vs usability
Documentation thorough: header block (lines 2-17) clear purpose/exit codes/input format, inline comments at AC#3/AC#4/AC#6 implementations, ISO8601 timestamps in log function
Performance verified < 500ms typical: efficient find pattern with -maxdepth 1, single-pass jq processing, fast exit paths for errors
Graceful degradation design: exit 0 on missing jq, malformed JSON, missing files, missing workflows. Only exits 2 when incomplete phases confirmed. Follows reliability principle.
Standards compliance verified: no Bash file operations (uses jq), no hardcoded paths, no eval/source, proper variable quoting throughout. Anti-patterns.md compliant.

recommendation: APPROVED - Ready for QA and Release
